name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-19
title: TDD subagent prompt is missing the spec-update transition step
description: The assembled subagent prompt transitions via tdd-step.py to test-red (Step 5), impl (Step 6), and test-green (Step 6). But the spec's forward chain is spec -> spec-update -> test-red -> impl -> test-green. The subagent never explicitly advances state to 'spec-update' before attempting test-red. If the feature starts at 'test-green' (typical post-merge state) the very first transition call (transition X test-red) goes BACKWARD and fails without --force. There is no --force in the prompt, and the SKILL.md commits spec changes before dispatch so the spec-update gate would be passable — but the state must first BE spec-update for the test-red transition to be a forward move. Fix: either explicitly add a 'transition <dir> spec-update' step before LOCK, or assume the dispatcher/spec authoring already pushed the state to spec-update. Spec must be explicit about the expected starting state.

history.note: Fixed: prompt now prepends spec-update transition before test-red. Note: subagent surfaced an inconsistency — actual tdd-step.py chain is test-green→deprecated, not test-green→spec-update. Filing follow-up backlog to reconcile spec vs state machine.
